model_narrative: We run an airline that must serve passenger demand on a set of routes using a fleet made up of several aircraft types. For each combination of aircraft type and route we decide how many aircraft of that type to fly on that route, and for each route we decide how much passenger demand to leave unserved by bumping it. Flying an aircraft on a route costs money that depends on the type and the route, and bumping passengers on a route carries a per-passenger penalty. The objective is to minimize the total of the flying costs and the bumping penalties across all routes.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the available aircraft types in the fleet

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the routes that must be served

params.name: c
params.doc: the operating cost of one aircraft of a given type flown on a given route, in thousands of dollars

params.name: pcap
params.doc: the passenger-carrying capacity of one aircraft of a given type on a given route, measured in hundreds of passengers

params.name: aircraft
params.doc: the number of aircraft of each type that are available to be assigned

params.name: fixeddemand
params.doc: the passenger demand that must be served on each route, measured in hundreds of passengers

params.name: costbumped
params.doc: the penalty cost incurred per unit of passenger demand that is bumped on a route rather than carried, in thousands of dollars per hundred passengers

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the number of aircraft of each type assigned to each route

vars.name: bumped
vars.doc: the amount of passenger demand bumped on each route, measured in hundreds of passengers

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the objective variable holding total cost

description: Each aircraft type has only so many aircraft available to fly. For each aircraft type, the total number of aircraft of that type assigned across all the routes cannot exceed the number of aircraft of that type that are available.

description: Every route has a passenger demand that has to be accounted for. For each route, the passengers that can be carried by the aircraft assigned to that route, together with the passengers that are bumped on that route, must be at least the demand on that route.
